page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor with 48 physical cores and 96 vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is provided via 3600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and the instance supports a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows strong multi-core CPU performance, with high marks in stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth is also highly rated, showing strong performance in both cached small-block and uncached large-block read and write operations. Database operations, including Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests, perform in the top 25% tier. However, single-core CPU performance and LLM text generation benchmarks are relatively weaker, with the 7B model text generation scoring in the bottom 10% due to the lack of dedicated GPU acceleration.

Given the high resource density of 768 GB RAM paired with 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 100 Gbps networking, this instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive and network-bound workloads. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, high-throughput web serving, and data-intensive processing pipelines. It is less suitable for workloads requiring high single-threaded performance or LLM text generation.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized, storage-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 768.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core CPU capabilities, high memory bandwidth, and excellent performance in database and Redis operations. However, single-core performance is average, and LLM text generation speeds are weak to poor due to the absence of hardware accelerators. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive databases, in-memory caching, and high-throughput web serving workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps networking, designed for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.24xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD, and 100 Gbps networking.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe storage, 100 Gbps networking, and strong multi-core performance for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 96 vCPUs and 768 GB of DDR4 RAM
3600 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Strong performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations
Suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and high-volume web serving
Lower efficiency for single-threaded tasks and LLM text generation
